summary: High‑energy May ’77 show with a stellar second set (Help > Slipknot! > Franklin’s, Eyes → Wharf Rat → Terrapin → Morning Dew) captured on a decent cassette audience tape.
review: Fans rank this performance among the very best of the legendary May 1977 run, praising the seamless transitions and Jerry Garcia’s vocal‑centric lead on the closing sequence. The recording is an audience cassette copy – not pristine but surprisingly clear and superior to the commercial release, preserving songs omitted from Dick’s Picks 3.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for the musical performance (multiple 5‑star reviews) and a generally favorable view of the cassette sound quality, yielding an overall rating in the good‑to‑great range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Cassette copy – not studio‑grade but clear enough; includes songs omitted from official releases and presents a full, unedited set.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is highlighted as superior, with seamless transitions (Eyes → Wharf Rat → Terrapin → Morning Dew) and a powerful Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s sequence; overall pacing keeps momentum high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia takes center stage vocally on the closing jam, his guitar tone is praised and his interplay with the rhythm section during Wharf Rat is highlighted as especially compelling.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers are noted for kicking butt on Wharf Rat, providing strong support to the ballad dynamics.
